subject: helm chart for so containers
commit_message:
helm chart for so containers

This check-in is an updated version of helm charts for so
containers based on review comments

Change-Id: I690430622ecbdcaf099a6165844142841db436dd
Issue-ID: SO-709
Signed-off-by: Ramesh Parthasarathy(rp6768)<rp6768@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(10 comments)

I started reviewing this commit, but there are just too many comments.
Please take a look at other components charts and improve accordingly.

Patch Set 7:

I don't have OOM setup in my machine. I just perform a --dry-run with helm. Please bear with me till I fully setup my machine.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 8:

Issue: Error: UPGRADE FAILED: Could not get information about the resource: resource name may not be empty.
I have addressed this issue now with patchset 8.

Patch Set 8:

Question: Do we have a port range for different application both for nodeport and internal ports? Please let me know

Patch Set 8:

Helm charts for so work correctly (including make all & linting). When I tried helm install with my VM, I got an error "release onap not found" & my VM ran out of space. I believe that this error is not related to so.
I would need some guidance in configuring so application to use msb. Please share any resources related to msb. I think this is the last piece to make "so" work correctly as expected.

This might be a flawed test since I turned off AAF to reduce helm chart size to get ONAP to start

Patch Set 10:

Brian Freeman helped me setup an environment to test these so helm charts. This patch includes all the fixes for a successful helm deployment. I need to work with Rob Daugherty as the application is throwing "CONFIG ERROR: APP environment variable not set" error. I'll let the team know about the outcome of my discussion with Rob.
Brian's help is very much appreciated.
Patch Set 11: Patch Set 10 was rebased

Patch Set 11: Verified-1

This does not deploy for me in 2 of my environments.  I have a k8s 1.8 and k8s 1.10 and it fails with the same thing in both:

error: release beast failed: ConfigMap in version "v1" cannot be handled as a ConfigMap: v1.ConfigMap: Data: ReadMapCB: expect { or n, parsing 27 ...","data":"... at {"apiVersion":"v1","data":"LOG_PATH:./logs/reqdb/","kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"name":"beast-so-request-db-adapter-configmap","namespace":"onap"}}


helm upgrade -i dev-so so --namespace onap --debug
[debug] Created tunnel using local port: '45290'

[debug] SERVER: "127.0.0.1:45290"

Release "dev-so" does not exist. Installing it now.
[debug] CHART PATH: /home/mkhinda/onap-gerrit/oom/kubernetes/so

Error: release dev-so failed: ConfigMap in version "v1" cannot be handled as a ConfigMap: v1.ConfigMap.Data: ReadMapCB: expect { or n, but found ", error found in #10 byte of ...|","data":"LOG_PATH:.|..., bigger context ...|{"apiVersion":"v1","data":"LOG_PATH:./logs/reqdb/","kind":"ConfigMap","metada|...


Something strange is going on with your configmaps....I tried to debug but didn't make much progress.  I see a lot of complexity and fancy stuff (cool btw!) in your charts so that might make troubleshooting a bit more difficult.

Did you see these problems when you tested with Brian?
Uploaded patch set 12.

Patch Set 12:

I have submitted patch 12. One of the issues I have been facing with MariaDB container is that it doesn't seem to create the catalogdb & requestdb even though scripts exist in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d directory. I managed to create these databases and most of the containers seemed to work correctly before environment had access issues. I will work on verification tomorrow and let the team know. Please start the verification after you hear from me. Thanks

so/values.yaml:    nodePort: 58
clamp/values.yaml:  nodePort2: 58

Patch Set 15:

30258 port conflict with pomba - (previously reserved by logdemonode) - see
